scenario: A fintech platform publishes 12000 messages per day from a Spring Boot service to Camunda 8 message catch events. While moving to the 8.8 starter, the team must migrate the deprecated zeebe.client.message properties and keep both the max message size of 4 MB and a time-to-live of 1 hour aligned with the documented replacements.
question: Which renaming pair matches the documented replacements for the deprecated zeebe.client.message properties?

options.text: Use camunda.client.max-message-size as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.max-message-size, and camunda.client.message-time-to-live as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.time-to-live

Use camunda.client.message.max-message-size as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.max-message-size, and camunda.client.message.time-to-live as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.time-to-live under a message group

Use camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-message-size as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.max-message-size, and camunda.client.worker.defaults.message-time-to-live for zeebe.client.message.time-to-live
Use camunda.client.cloud.max-message-size as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.max-message-size, and camunda.client.cloud.message-time-to-live for zeebe.client.message.time-to-live

a.text: Correct. The zeebe.client.message deprecation table lists camunda.client.max-message-size (env CAMUNDA_CLIENT_MAXMESSAGESIZE) as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.max-message-size, and camunda.client.message-time-to-live as the replacement for zeebe.client.message.time-to-live.

b.text: Incorrect. The replacements live directly under camunda.client (as max-message-size and message-time-to-live), not under a camunda.client.message subgroup.

c.text: Incorrect. Worker defaults cover properties like name, type, poll-interval, and timeout; max message size and message TTL move under camunda.client, not under camunda.client.worker.defaults.

d.text: Incorrect. The cloud namespace covers cluster-id, region, and domain; message size and TTL do not belong to camunda.client.cloud in the deprecation tables.

explanation: The zeebe.client.message deprecation table maps max-message-size to camunda.client.max-message-size and time-to-live to camunda.client.message-time-to-live. Both replacements sit directly under the camunda.client root rather than within a message subgroup.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
